name: Lorentz invariance of photon propagation
requirement: Any candidate framework must respect Lorentz invariance to the precision set by astrophysical and laboratory tests. The most stringent kinematic test comes from the energy-dependent dispersion of photons over cosmological distances: high-energy and low-energy gamma-ray photons from a single gamma-ray burst arrive within constraints implying a quantum-gravity scale E_QG well above the Planck mass for linear-in-energy modifications, and well above the Planck mass squared for quadratic-in-energy modifications.

regime.description: GRB photons in the GeV-TeV range, propagating over Gpc distances.

current_bound.value: E_QG^(linear) > 7.6 x M_Planck (lower limit on linear-in-energy LIV scale)
current_bound.reference: Vasileiou et al., 'Constraints on Lorentz Invariance Violation from Fermi-Large Area Telescope Observations of Gamma-Ray Bursts', PRD 87, 122001 (2013), arXiv:1305.3463

what_it_excludes: Frameworks predicting an energy-dependent photon propagation speed at the linear-in-E/M_Planck level - including some 'phenomenological quantum gravity' ansatze and certain non-commutative-spacetime models. Quadratic-suppression models remain viable.
notes: Many other LIV channels exist (electron synchrotron edges, vacuum birefringence from polarized AGN photons, neutrino time-of-flight, threshold anomalies in cosmic-ray propagation). Each places independent bounds. A serious candidate framework must pass all of them; this entry uses the GRB photon-dispersion bound as the most readily-quoted single number.

primary_references.citation: Vasileiou et al., 'Constraints on Lorentz Invariance Violation from Fermi-Large Area Telescope Observations of Gamma-Ray Bursts', PRD 87, 122001 (2013)
Mattingly, 'Modern Tests of Lorentz Invariance', Living Rev. Relativ. 8, 5 (2005)
Kostelecky and Russell, 'Data Tables for Lorentz and CPT Violation', Rev. Mod. Phys. 83, 11 (2011); annual updates at arXiv:0801.0287
